text.title: Visibility of System Status Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Visibility of System Status. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every UI control responds within 100ms of activation (visual change).
rule.rationale: Every UI control responds within 100ms of activation (visual change).

rule.statement: Actions >1s show a progress indicator (spinner, bar, or step).
rule.rationale: Actions >1s show a progress indicator (spinner, bar, or step).

rule.statement: Every async outcome (success, failure) is announced visually + via aria-live polite.
rule.rationale: Every async outcome (success, failure) is announced visually + via aria-live polite.

rule.statement: Status is communicated via icon + text + colour, not colour alone.
rule.rationale: Status is communicated via icon + text + colour, not colour alone.

rule.statement: Failed actions surface within 5s of failure with cause + recovery action.
rule.rationale: Failed actions surface within 5s of failure with cause + recovery action.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-visibility-of-system-status.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
